meta.title: Dr. Jorge Arce — The Arce Experience · v16 HandX
meta.description: The Arce Experience — oral & maxillofacial surgery as a guided journey. Dental Implants Center of South Bay.

sections.date:
Dental Implants Center of South Bay
Consultations by appointment
sections.title: The Arce <t>Experience</t>

sections.label: The Mission
sections.quote: “Before the procedure, the person. <t>Difficult work, done with care,</t> gives people their lives back — that conviction has guided twenty-seven years of surgery.”
sections.who: Dr. Jorge Arce — Board-Certified Oral & Maxillofacial Surgeon

days.title: An honest <t>conversation.</t>
days.body: A long, unhurried first visit. Your history, your scans, your questions — and an honest read of what is possible. If the right answer is not to operate, you will hear that plainly.

days.title: The case, <t>mapped.</t>
days.body: Every case is planned before anyone operates — the time the case actually needs, not the time the schedule allows. Twenty-seven years of judgment applied to your anatomy.

days.title: Steady <t>hands.</t>
days.body: Precision in the operating room, calm around it. From single implants to full-arch All-On-X reconstruction — built on structural, reconstructive rigor.

days.title: Function, structure, <t>confidence.</t>
days.body: Restoration and long-term maintenance that protect the work — and a full report back to your referring dentist, every time. The smile is the last step, not the first promise.

rows.d: Trained in Colombia, built in California — a career devoted entirely to oral & maxillofacial surgery and implantology.
Take the time the case actually needs — the unglamorous principle behind twenty-seven years of results.
Every step explained until it is understood. Patients consistently describe feeling calm, informed, and heard.
Much of the practice arrives by referral — and goes back with a full surgical report, every time.

cards.q: “I had been told by two surgeons it was impossible. Dr. Arce took the time no one else did. <t>I can eat, smile, and speak again.”</t>
“When a case is beyond what I can handle, Jorge is the first call I make.”
“The most thorough and honest surgeon I have ever met. <t>He treated my father like family.”</t>
“Calm, precise, and genuinely kind. He explained every step until I understood.”
“I refer my most complex cases to Dr. Arce without hesitation. <t>Twenty-seven years shows.”</t>
“Years of pain, gone. I only wish I had found him sooner.”

cards.title: First <t>Consultation</t>
items: Unhurried review of history and imaging
An honest read of what is possible
A clear plan, in plain language

cards.title: Second <t>Opinion</t>
cards.for: For patients told it can't be done
items: A fresh look at a complex diagnosis
Including, when right, the advice not to operate

cards.title: By <t>Referral</t>
items: The complex cases you'd rather not carry alone
A full surgical report back, every time
Restorative work stays with your practice

rows.q: What happens at a first consultation?
rows.a: A long, unhurried conversation. Dr. Arce reviews your history and imaging, examines the site, and gives you an honest read of what is possible — including, when it is the right answer, the recommendation not to operate.

rows.q: I was told my case is impossible. Is it?
rows.a: Sometimes it is — and you deserve to hear that plainly. But many of the cases in this practice arrived with that exact sentence. Complex cases are not a detour from this work; they are the work.

rows.q: How do I know if I'm a candidate?
rows.a: Bone volume, gum health, medical history, and timing all matter. The free patient guide covers the signs — and a consultation settles it definitively with imaging.

rows.q: Do you work with referring dentists?
rows.a: Constantly — much of this practice arrives by referral. Every referred patient goes back with a full surgical report, and the restorative work stays with your general dentist.
